content.problemStatement:
Coach Khaled is a swag teacher in HIT (Hag Institute of Technology). However, he has some obsession problems.

Recently, coach Khaled was teaching a course in building 8G networks using TV antennas and programming them with assembly. There are NNN students (numbered 111 through NNN) in his class; for some reason, this number is always a multiple of 444. The final exam has finished and Khaled has all the scores of his NNN students. For each valid iii, the score of the iii-th student is AiA_iAi​; each score is an integer between 000 and 100100100. Currently, the score-grade distribution is as follows:


grade D for score smaller than 606060
grade C for score greater or equal to 606060, but smaller than 757575
grade B for score greater or equal to 757575, but smaller than 909090
grade A for score greater or equal to 909090
However, coach Khaled is not satisfied with this. He wants exactly N/4N/4N/4 students to receive each grade (A, B, C and D), so that the grades are perfectly balanced. The scores cannot be changed, but the boundaries between grades can. Therefore, he wants to choose three integers xxx, yyy and zzz and change the grade distribution to the following (note that initially, x=60x = 60x=60, y=75y = 75y=75 and z=90z = 90z=90):


grade D for score smaller than xxx
grade C for score greater or equal to xxx, but smaller than yyy
grade B for score greater or equal to yyy, but smaller than zzz
grade A for score greater or equal to zzz
Your task is to find thresholds xxx, yyy and zzz that result in a perfect balance of grades. If there are multiple solutions, choose the one with the maximum value of x+y+zx+y+zx+y+z (because coach Khaled wants seem smarter than his students); it can be proved that there is at most one such solution. Sometimes, there is no way to choose the thresholds and coach Khaled would resign because his exam questions were low-quality.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, if there is no solution, print a single line containing the integer −1-1−1; otherwise, print a single line containing three space-separated integers xxx, yyy and zzz.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The values x=60,y=75x = 60, y = 75x=60,y=75 and z=90z = 90z=90 satisfy all the conditions. Each grade is received by one student each.

The student with score 252525 gets grade D.
The student with score 606060 gets grade C.
The student with score 757575 gets grade B.
The student with score 909090 gets grade A.

Test case 222: It can be proven that there exists no tuple that satisfies all the conditions.
Test case 333: The values x=1,y=2x = 1, y = 2x=1,y=2 and z=3z = 3z=3 satisfy all the conditions. Each grade is received by one student each.

The student with score 000 gets grade D.
The student with score 111 gets grade C.
The student with score 222 gets grade B.
The student with score 333 gets grade A.

Test case 444: It can be proven that there exists no tuple that satisfies all the conditions.
